div.head: The Ambassador in China (Stuart) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Nanking, April 17, 1948—2 p. m.
[Received April 17—5:32 a. m.]

div.p: 699. Tsai Ting-kaiMember of the Kuomintang Revolutionary Committee. in strictest confidence today asserted T. V. Soong told Marshal Li [Chi-shen] during recent confidential interview that Government probably will collapse in about 3 months and expressed desire to cooperate with Kmt Revolutionary Committee immediately following Chiang’s loss of power. Tsai also asserted Mme. Sun Yat-senSister of Madame Chiang Kai-shek and T. V. Soong. actively working for Kmt and has obtained wide support in military, political and educational circles. Tsai implied Li Chi-shen expects to stage military revolt within 6 months, but was impressed with Soong’s estimate.

Above sent Embassy by Hong Kong April 13. On my recent visit
Canton, Soong gave no indication that he felt collapse of Government is imminent. However, he stated that he had recently seen Tsai Ting-kai and that he felt Tsai would cooperate with him if the Central Government were to disintegrate.
